issued_sql:
-- Profile shipments grain: is shipment_id truly 1 row per shipment, or do orders have multiple shipments?
select 
  count(*) as shipment_rows,
  count(distinct shipment_id) as distinct_shipment_ids,
  count(distinct order_id) as distinct_order_ids
from analytics.stg_shipments;

-- Compare grain: orders per order_id in stg_orders vs shipments per order_id
with s as (select order_id, count(*) as rows_per_order from analytics.stg_shipments group by order_id)
select 
  count(*) as orders_with_shipments,
  count(*) filter (where rows_per_order > 1) as orders_with_multiple_shipments,
  max(rows_per_order) as max_shipments_per_order,
  avg(rows_per_order)::numeric(6,2) as avg_shipments_per_order
from s;

-- Reconcile mart_revenue_daily orders_count vs direct from int_orders_joined vs stg_orders
select 
  (select count(*) from analytics.mart_revenue_daily where date_day >= '2026-06-27') as mart_total,
  (select count(*) from analytics.int_orders_joined where ordered_at::date >= '2026-06-27') as int_total,
  (select count(*) from analytics.stg_orders where ordered_at::date >= '2026-06-27') as stg_orders_total;

-- Compare daily fanout ratio before vs after onset
with int as (
  select ordered_at::date as d, order_id from analytics.int_orders_joined
),
sto as (
  select ordered_at::date as d, order_id from analytics.stg_orders
)
select 
  int.d as date_day,
  (select count(distinct order_id) from sto where d = int.d) as true_orders,
  (select count(*) from int where d = int.d) as int_rows,
  round(
    (select count(*) from int where d = int.d)::numeric 
    / nullif((select count(distinct order_id) from sto where d = int.d), 0), 3
  ) as fanout_ratio
from (select distinct d from int) int
where int.d between '2026-06-20' and '2026-07-10'
order by int.d;

-- Check raw_shipments: did multiple-shipments-per-order behavior start at onset?
select 
  case when shipped_at::date >= '2026-06-27' then 'post' else 'pre' end as period,
  count(*) as shipment_rows,
  count(distinct order_id) as distinct_orders,
  count(*) - count(distinct order_id) as fanout_excess,
  round((count(*)::numeric / nullif(count(distinct order_id),0)) - 1, 4) as fanout_pct
from analytics.stg_shipments
where shipped_at::date between '2026-06-20' and '2026-07-10'
group by 1;

-- Verify: aggregating shipments to order-grain in mart reproduces correct counts
-- Show the daily mismatch and what a corrected mart would compute
with ship_per_order as (
  select order_id, count(*) as shipment_n, sum(shipping_cost) as ship_cost
  from analytics.stg_shipments
  group by order_id
)
select 
  o.ordered_at::date as date_day,
  count(*) as corrected_orders,
  sum(coalesce(p.order_revenue, 0)) - sum(coalesce(r.refund_amount, 0)) as corrected_net
from analytics.stg_orders o
left join (select order_id, sum(amount) as order_revenue from analytics.stg_payments group by order_id) p on p.order_id = o.order_id
left join (select order_id, sum(amount) as refund_amount from analytics.stg_refunds group by order_id) r on r.order_id = o.order_id
where o.ordered_at::date between '2026-06-20' and '2026-07-10'
group by 1
order by 1;

-- Confirm exact mart vs corrected daily
select date_day, orders_count as mart_orders from analytics.mart_revenue_daily 
where date_day between '2026-06-20' and '2026-07-10' order by date_day;

select date_day, orders_count, total_revenue, refunds_amount, net_revenue, aov
from analytics.mart_revenue_daily
where date_day >= '2026-06-20' and date_day <= '2026-07-10'
order by date_day;

-- Check int_payment_reconciliation: counts of anomalies by date
select 
    date_trunc('day', o.ordered_at)::date as d,
    count(*) as orders,
    sum(case when r.refund_exceeds_revenue then 1 else 0 end) as refund_exceeds,
    sum(case when r.no_successful_payment then 1 else 0 end) as no_payment,
    sum(case when r.payment_status_mismatch then 1 else 0 end) as mismatch
from analytics.int_orders_joined o
join analytics.int_payment_reconciliation r using (order_id)
where o.ordered_at >= '2026-06-20'
group by 1
order by 1;

-- Check distinct orders in int_orders_joined vs stg_orders around onset
-- And check shipment duplication (since the join LEFTs onto shipments)
select 
    date_trunc('day', o.ordered_at)::date as d,
    count(distinct o.order_id) as distinct_orders,
    count(*) as rows_in_int,
    count(*) - count(distinct o.order_id) as dup_rows
from analytics.int_orders_joined o
where o.ordered_at >= '2026-06-20'
group by 1
order by 1;

-- Check raw_refunds vs stg_refunds - is the refunds source itself dropping?
select 'raw' as src, date_trunc('day', refunded_at)::date as d, count(*) as rows, sum(amount) as total
from raw.raw_refunds where refunded_at >= '2026-06-20' group by 1
union all
select 'stg' as src, date_trunc('day', refunded_at)::date as d, count(*) as rows, sum(amount) as total
from analytics.stg_refunds where refunded_at >= '2026-06-20' group by 1
order by d, src;

-- Check raw_payments vs stg_payments
select 'raw' as src, date_trunc('day', paid_at)::date as d, count(*) as rows, sum(amount) as total
from raw.raw_payments where paid_at >= '2026-06-20' group by 1
union all
select 'stg' as src, date_trunc('day', paid_at)::date as d, count(*) as rows, sum(amount) as total
from analytics.stg_payments where paid_at >= '2026-06-20' group by 1
order by d, src;

-- Why did refunds drop to 0? Check refunds counts and amounts over time
select 'raw' as src, refunded_at::date as d, count(*) as rows, sum(amount) as total
from raw.raw_refunds where refunded_at >= '2026-06-15' group by 1
union all
select 'stg' as src, refunded_at::date as d, count(*) as rows, sum(amount) as total
from analytics.stg_refunds where refunded_at >= '2026-06-15' group by 1
union all
select 'int_refunds' as src, refunded_at::date as d, count(*) as rows, sum(amount) as total
from (select order_id, sum(amount) as amount, max(refunded_at) as refunded_at from analytics.stg_refunds group by 1) f where refunded_at >= '2026-06-15' group by 1
order by d, src;

-- How many orders have NO matching payment at all (in stg_payments, by success status)?
-- I.e., how many order_ids in stg_orders have no corresponding row in stg_payments (success)?
-- Sample a couple of days
select 
    o.ordered_at::date as d,
    count(*) as total_orders,
    count(*) filter (where p.order_id is null) as orders_with_no_success_payment
from analytics.stg_orders o
left join analytics.stg_payments p on o.order_id = p.order_id
where o.ordered_at >= '2026-06-20'
group by 1
order by 1;

-- Look at the int_orders_joined output for 2026-06-28 specifically:
-- compare to a recompute of the same join from declared inputs
with payments as (
    select order_id, sum(amount) as order_revenue, count(*) as payment_count
    from analytics.stg_payments group by order_id
),
shipments as (select * from analytics.stg_shipments),
refunds as (select order_id, sum(amount) as refund_amount from analytics.stg_refunds group by order_id)
select 
    count(*) as rows,
    count(p.order_id) as with_payment,
    count(s.shipment_id) as with_shipment,
    count(r.order_id) as with_refund,
    count(*) filter (where p.order_revenue is null) as null_revenue
from analytics.stg_orders o
left join payments p on o.order_id = p.order_id
left join shipments s on o.order_id = s.order_id
left join refunds r on o.order_id = r.order_id
where o.ordered_at::date = '2026-06-28';

-- Now check the int_payment_reconciliation directly
select 
    date(o.ordered_at) as d,
    count(*) filter (where r.no_successful_payment) as no_pay_recon,
    count(*) filter (where r.refund_exceeds_revenue) as ref_exc,
    -- recompute no_successful_payment inline
    count(*) filter (where p.order_revenue is null) as recomputed_no_pay
from analytics.stg_orders o
left join (select order_id, sum(amount) as order_revenue from analytics.stg_payments group by 1) p on o.order_id = p.order_id
left join (select order_id, sum(amount) as refund_amount from analytics.stg_refunds group by 1) rf on o.order_id = rf.order_id
cross join lateral (
    select 
        o.order_id,
        p.order_revenue is null as no_successful_payment,
        coalesce(rf.refund_amount, 0) > coalesce(p.order_revenue, 0) as refund_exceeds_revenue
) r
where o.ordered_at >= '2026-06-25' and o.ordered_at < '2026-07-02'
group by 1
order by 1;

-- Investigate: in the int_orders_joined output for 2026-06-28, are there duplicate order_ids?
select 
    count(*) as rows,
    count(distinct o.order_id) as distinct_orders
from analytics.int_orders_joined o
where o.ordered_at::date = '2026-06-28';

-- Check shipments duplicates per order
select 
    order_id, count(*) as ship_rows
from analytics.stg_shipments
where order_id in (select order_id from analytics.stg_orders where ordered_at::date = '2026-06-28')
group by 1
order by ship_rows desc
limit 10;

-- Check payments duplicates per order
select 
    order_id, count(*) as pay_rows, sum(amount) as tot
from analytics.stg_payments
where order_id in (select order_id from analytics.stg_orders where ordered_at::date = '2026-06-28')
group by 1
order by pay_rows desc
limit 10;

-- Check refund duplicates per order
select 
    order_id, count(*) as ref_rows
from analytics.stg_refunds
where order_id in (select order_id from analytics.stg_orders where ordered_at::date = '2026-06-28')
group by 1
order by ref_rows desc
limit 10;

-- Look at the int_orders_joined output for an order_id with multiple payments (O028007)
-- to see if the join is correctly aggregating
select *
from analytics.int_orders_joined
where order_id = 'O028007';

-- I see — the count(p.order_id)=340 vs null_revenue=105 is because the join is multiplied.
-- Let me show 5 rows from int_orders_joined for 2026-06-28 that have null revenue
select order_id, customer_id, order_revenue, payment_count, refund_amount, shipment_id
from analytics.int_orders_joined
where ordered_at::date = '2026-06-28' and order_revenue is null
limit 5;

-- For those 5 orders, do they have any successful payment in stg_payments?
select o.order_id, p.order_id as p_oid, p.amount, p.status
from analytics.stg_orders o
left join analytics.stg_payments p on o.order_id = p.order_id
where o.ordered_at::date = '2026-06-28' and o.order_id in (
    select order_id from analytics.int_orders_joined 
    where ordered_at::date = '2026-06-28' and order_revenue is null
    limit 5
);

-- Count payments with status=success but amount null, by paid_at date
select paid_at::date as d, count(*) as null_amount_success
from analytics.stg_payments
where status = 'success' and amount is null
group by 1
order by 1;

-- In raw_payments, same check
select paid_at::date as d, count(*) as null_amount_success
from raw.raw_payments
where status = 'success' and amount is null
group by 1
order by 1;

-- Final recon: what would total_revenue have been on 2026-06-28 if these null amounts were 0
-- vs if they were the actual amounts (we don't have the amount), so the best we can do is
-- quantify: 105 of 346 orders missing revenue. Show a few sample amounts from before vs after
-- to quantify the magnitude of the defect.
select 
    'before (2026-06-20..27)' as period,
    round(avg(total_revenue), 0) as avg_total,
    round(avg(orders_count), 0) as avg_orders,
    round(avg(total_revenue)/avg(orders_count), 2) as aov
from analytics.mart_revenue_daily where date_day between '2026-06-20' and '2026-06-27'
union all
select 
    'after (2026-06-28..07-10)' as period,
    round(avg(total_revenue), 0) as avg_total,
    round(avg(orders_count), 0) as avg_orders,
    round(avg(total_revenue)/avg(orders_count), 2) as aov
from analytics.mart_revenue_daily where date_day between '2026-06-28' and '2026-07-10';
blocked_sql:
-- Why did refunds drop to 0? Check refunds counts and amounts over time
select 'raw' as src, refunded_at::date as d, count(*) as rows, sum(amount) as total
from raw.raw_refunds where refunded_at >= '2026-06-15' group by 1
union all
select 'stg' as src, refunded_at::date as d, count(*) as rows, sum(amount) as total
from analytics.stg_refunds where refunded_at >= '2026-06-15' group by 1
union all
select 'int_refunds' as src, refunded_at::date as d, count(*) as rows, sum(amount) as total
from (select order_id, sum(amount) as amount, max(refunded_at) as refunded_at from analytics.stg_refunds group by 1) f where refunded_at >= '2026-06-15' group by 1
order by d, src;

issued_sql:
select date_day, orders_count, total_revenue, refunds_amount, net_revenue, aov
from analytics.mart_revenue_daily
where date_day between '2026-06-20' and '2026-07-05'
order by date_day;

-- grain check: rows per order_id in stg_shipments
select order_id, count(*) as n
from analytics.stg_shipments
group by order_id
having count(*) > 1
order by n desc
limit 20;

-- grain check: rows per order_id in stg_refunds and stg_payments
select order_id, count(*) as n
from analytics.stg_refunds
group by order_id
having count(*) > 1
limit 5;
select 'payments' as src, order_id, count(*) as n
from analytics.stg_payments
group by order_id
having count(*) > 1
limit 5;

-- Profile int_orders_joined around onset: null rate of order_revenue, by date
select ordered_at::date as d,
       count(*) as orders,
       count(*) filter (where order_revenue is null) as no_payment_orders,
       round(count(*) filter (where order_revenue is null)::numeric/count(*), 3) as no_payment_rate,
       sum(coalesce(order_revenue,0)) as total_revenue,
       round(avg(coalesce(order_revenue,0))::numeric, 2) as avg_rev
from analytics.int_orders_joined
where ordered_at between '2026-06-20' and '2026-07-05'
group by 1
order by 1;

-- Profile stg_payments: null rate of amount, by paid_at date
select paid_at::date as d,
       count(*) as payment_rows,
       count(*) filter (where amount is null) as null_amount_rows
from analytics.stg_payments
where paid_at between '2026-06-20' and '2026-07-05'
group by 1
order by 1;

-- And directly against raw_payments to localize
select paid_at::date as d,
       count(*) as rows,
       count(*) filter (where amount is null) as null_amount_rows
from raw.raw_payments
where paid_at between '2026-06-20' and '2026-07-05'
group by 1
order by 1;

-- Final confirmation: a stg_payments sample showing NULL amounts post-onset
select payment_id, order_id, amount, payment_method, status, paid_at
from analytics.stg_payments
where amount is null and paid_at >= '2026-06-28'
order by paid_at
limit 5;

select date_day, orders_count, total_revenue, refunds_amount, net_revenue, aov
from analytics.mart_revenue_daily
where date_day between '2026-06-20' and '2026-07-10'
order by date_day;

-- grain check: rows per order_id in stg_shipments, stg_payments (success), stg_refunds
select 'stg_shipments' as src, count(*) rows_total, count(distinct order_id) distinct_orders,
       count(*) - count(distinct order_id) as fanout_rows,
       max(c) as max_per_order
from (select order_id, count(*) c from analytics.stg_shipments group by order_id) t
union all
select 'stg_payments', count(*), count(distinct order_id),
       count(*) - count(distinct order_id),
       max(c)
from (select order_id, count(*) c from analytics.stg_payments group by order_id) t
union all
select 'stg_refunds', count(*), count(distinct order_id),
       count(*) - count(distinct order_id),
       max(c)
from (select order_id, count(*) c from analytics.stg_refunds group by order_id) t;

-- true grain: rows vs distinct keys
select 'stg_shipments' as src, count(*) rows_total, count(distinct order_id) distinct_orders
from analytics.stg_shipments
union all
select 'stg_payments', count(*), count(distinct order_id) from analytics.stg_payments
union all
select 'stg_refunds', count(*), count(distinct order_id) from analytics.stg_refunds
union all
select 'stg_orders', count(*), count(distinct order_id) from analytics.stg_orders;

-- Does the +21% alert onset 2026-06-27 reconcile with row counts in stg_orders?
select date(ordered_at) d, count(*) rows, count(distinct order_id) distinct_orders
from analytics.stg_orders
where ordered_at >= '2026-06-20' and ordered_at < '2026-07-11'
group by 1 order by 1;

-- Mart around onset
select date_day, orders_count, total_revenue, refunds_amount, net_revenue, aov
from analytics.mart_revenue_daily
where date_day between date '2026-06-20' and date '2026-07-05'
order by date_day;

-- Check int_orders_joined grain: rows per order_id before vs after onset
select case when ordered_at::date < date '2026-06-28' then 'pre' else 'post' end as period,
       count(*) as rows,
       count(distinct order_id) as distinct_orders,
       count(*)*1.0/nullif(count(distinct order_id),0) as rows_per_order
from analytics.int_orders_joined
where ordered_at::date between date '2026-06-20' and date '2026-07-05'
group by 1;

-- Per-side fan-out diagnostic on int_orders_joined: which join multiplies?
with j as (select * from analytics.int_orders_joined
           where ordered_at::date between date '2026-06-20' and date '2026-07-05')
select case when ordered_at::date < date '2026-06-28' then 'pre' else 'post' end as period,
       count(*) as rows,
       count(distinct order_id) as distinct_orders,
       count(*) filter (where shipment_id is not null) as rows_with_shipment,
       count(*) filter (where refund_amount is not null) as rows_with_refund,
       count(*) filter (where order_revenue is not null) as rows_with_payment,
       max(coalesce(shipment_id::text,'-')) as sample_ship
from j
group by 1;

-- Per-order sum in stg_payments vs int_orders_joined.order_revenue, before vs after
with p as (
  select order_id, sum(amount) s, count(*) n
  from analytics.stg_payments
  where paid_at::date between date '2026-06-20' and date '2026-07-05'
  group by order_id
),
j as (
  select order_id, order_revenue, payment_count
  from analytics.int_orders_joined
  where ordered_at::date between date '2026-06-20' and date '2026-07-05')
select case when (select min(ordered_at::date) from analytics.int_orders_joined where order_id=j.order_id) < date '2026-06-28' then 'pre' else 'post' end period,
       count(*) orders,
       sum(p.s) stg_sum_amt, sum(j.order_revenue) int_sum_amt, sum(p.n) stg_n, sum(j.payment_count) int_n,
       round(sum(j.order_revenue)/nullif(sum(p.s),0),3) ratio
from j left join p using(order_id)
group by 1;

-- Distribution of payment amounts pre vs post
select case when paid_at::date < date '2026-06-28' then 'pre' else 'post' end period,
       count(*) rows, count(distinct order_id) orders,
       min(amount), max(amount), avg(amount), sum(amount),
       percentile_cont(0.5) within group (order by amount) as med
from analytics.stg_payments
where paid_at::date between date '2026-06-20' and date '2026-07-05'
group by 1;

-- Look at raw payments column types and a few sample rows post-onset
select * from raw.raw_payments
where paid_at::date >= date '2026-06-28' and paid_at::date <= date '2026-07-02'
order by paid_at limit 30;

-- Tail of stg_payments post-onset: top amounts and their currency/payment_method
select amount, payment_method, status, count(*) n
from analytics.stg_payments
where paid_at::date >= date '2026-06-28' and paid_at::date <= date '2026-07-05'
group by 1,2,3
order by amount desc
limit 25;

-- Check raw_payments for new payment_methods or status values post-onset
select payment_method, status, count(*) n, min(amount) mn, max(amount) mx, avg(amount) av
from raw.raw_payments
where paid_at::date >= date '2026-06-28' and paid_at::date <= date '2026-07-05'
group by 1,2
order by 3 desc;

-- Is there a currency column on raw_payments / stg_payments? Inspect schema
select column_name, data_type from information_schema.columns
where table_schema='raw' and table_name='raw_payments'
order by ordinal_position;

-- Quantify: bank_transfer contribution to post-onset total_revenue in mart
select sum(case when payment_method='bank_transfer' and status='success' then amount else 0 end) bank_success_amt,
       sum(case when payment_method='bank_transfer' and status='failed' then amount else 0 end) bank_failed_amt,
       sum(case when payment_method='bank_transfer' and status='pending' then amount else 0 end) bank_pending_amt,
       sum(amount) total_amt
from raw.raw_payments
where paid_at::date >= date '2026-06-28' and paid_at::date <= date '2026-07-05';

-- Reconcile: does int_orders_joined's sum(order_revenue) match stg_payments sum on the same period?
-- (Use mart_/int - confirm no mart-side arithmetic error.)
select (select sum(order_revenue) from analytics.int_orders_joined where ordered_at::date between date '2026-06-28' and date '2026-07-05') as int_sum_revenue,
       (select sum(total_revenue) from analytics.mart_revenue_daily where date_day between date '2026-06-28' and date '2026-07-05') as mart_sum_revenue;

-- And confirm bank_transfer is the *new* phenomenon: absent pre-onset?
select min(paid_at) min_paid, max(paid_at) max_paid, count(*) n
from raw.raw_payments
where payment_method='bank_transfer';

-- Did bank_transfer amounts always run in the $thousands, or only post-onset?
select case when paid_at::date < date '2026-06-28' then 'pre' else 'post' end period,
       count(*) n, count(*) filter (where status='success') s_n,
       round(avg(amount)::numeric,2) avg_amt,
       min(amount) mn, max(amount) mx
from raw.raw_payments
where payment_method='bank_transfer'
group by 1;

-- And the date the bank_transfer high-amount rows begin:
select date_trunc('day', paid_at)::date d, count(*) n, round(avg(amount)::numeric,0) avg_amt, max(amount) mx
from raw.raw_payments
where payment_method='bank_transfer' and amount > 500
group by 1 order by 1;
select ordered_at::date as date_day, count(*) as orders_count from analytics.int_orders_joined group by 1 order by 1
select date_day, orders_count from analytics.mart_revenue_daily order by date_day

select 'before_onset' as period, count(*) as rows, count(distinct order_id) as distinct_orders, count(*) - count(distinct order_id) as extra_rows from analytics.int_orders_joined where ordered_at::date < '2026-06-27'
union all
select 'after_onset', count(*), count(distinct order_id), count(*) - count(distinct order_id) from analytics.int_orders_joined where ordered_at::date >= '2026-06-27'

select 'before_onset' as period, count(*) as rows, count(distinct order_id) as distinct_orders from analytics.stg_shipments where shipped_at::date < '2026-06-27' or shipped_at is null
union all
select 'after_onset', count(*), count(distinct order_id) from analytics.stg_shipments where shipped_at::date >= '2026-06-27'
select order_id, count(*) as shipment_rows from analytics.stg_shipments where shipped_at::date >= '2026-06-27' group by 1 having count(*) > 1 order by shipment_rows desc limit 5
select ordered_at::date, count(*) as raw_orders from raw.raw_orders where ordered_at::date between '2026-06-26' and '2026-06-30' group by 1 order by 1
